records.chapter: Reward of reciting salutation (Salawaat) on the Holy Prophet (s.a.w.s.) loudly
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1.  Imam  Ja’far  Sadiq  (a.s.)  narrates  that  the  Holy  Prophet (s.a.w.s.) said, “Recite salutation loudly, for it keeps away hypocrisy from you.”
records.arabicText: وَ بِهَذَا الْإِسْنَادِ عَنِ الْحُسَیْنِ بْنِ یَزِیدَ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ سِنَانٍ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ص ارْفَعُوا أَصْوَاتَکُمْ بِالصَّلَاهِ عَلَیَّ فَإِنَّهَا تَذْهَبُ بِالنِّفَاقِ.

records.chapter: Reward of reciting Sub-h’aanallaah a hundredntimes after Morning Prayer and Allaahumma ihdinee after every Prayer
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to us by Sa’d b. Abdillah from Ahmad b. Musa from al-Hussain b. Sa’eed from Ibn Abi ’Umayr from Muawiyah b. Wahab from ’Amru b. Yazid from Salim al-Makki from Abi Ja’far (a.s.) who said: “If he appears on the Day of Judgment without leaving these actions purposely after every prayer Allah will open all eight doors of Paradise for him and he will be free to enter Paradise from whichever door he wishes.”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا سَعْدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُوسَی عَنِ الْحُسَیْنِ بْنِ سَعِیدٍ عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِی عُمَیْرٍ عَنْ مُعَاوِیَهَ بْنِ وَهْبٍ عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ یَزِیدَ عَنْ سَالِمٍ الْمَکِّیِّ عَنْ أَبِی جَعْفَرٍ ع قَالَ: أَتَی النَّبِیَّ ص رَجُلٌ یُقَالُ لَهُ شَیْبَهُ الْهُذَلِیُّ فَقَالَ لَهُ یَا نَبِیَّ اللَّهِ إِنِّی شَیْخٌ کَبِیرٌ قَدْ کَبِرَتْ سِنِّی وَ ضَعُفَتْ قُوَّتِی عَمَّا کَانَ تَعَوَّدْتُهُ نَفْسِی مِنْ صَلَاهٍ وَ صِیَامٍ وَ حَجٍّ وَ جِهَادٍ فَعَلِّمْنِی یَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ کَلَاماً یَنْفَعُنِی اللَّهُ بِهِ وَ خَفِّفْ عَلَیَّ یَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ فَقَالَ أَعِدْ فَأَعَادَ ثَلَاثَ مَرَّاتٍ فَقَالَ لَهُ النَّبِیُّ ص مَا حَوْلَکَ شَجَرَهٌ وَ لَا مَدَرَهٌ إِلَّا قَدْ بَکَتْ مِنْ رَحْمَتِکَ فَإِذَا صَلَّیْتَ الصُّبْحَ فَقُلْ عَشْرَ مَرَّاتٍ سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ الْعَظِیمِ وَ بِحَمْدِهِ وَ لَا حَوْلَ وَ لَا قُوَّهَ إِلَّا بِاللَّهِ الْعَلِیِّ الْعَظِیمِ فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ یُعَافِیکَ بِذَلِکَ مِنَ الْعَمَی وَ الْجُنُونِ وَ الْجُذَامِ وَ الْفَقْرِ وَ الْهَدْمِ فَقَالَ یَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ هَذَا لِلدُّنْیَا فَمَا لِلْآخِرَهِ قَالَ تَقُولُ فِی دُبُرِ کُلِّ صَلَاهٍ اللَّهُمَّ اهْدِنِی مِنْ عِنْدِکَ وَ أَفِضْ عَلَیَّ مِنْ فَضْلِکَ وَ انْشُرْ عَلَیَّ مِنْ رَحْمَتِکَ وَ أَنْزِلْ عَلَیَّ مِنْ بَرَکَاتِکَ قَالَ فَقَبَضَ عَلَیْهِنَّ بِیَدِهِ ثُمَّ مَضَی فَقَالَ النَّبِیُّ ص أَمَا إِنَّهُ إِنْ وَافَی یَوْمَ الْقِیَامَهِ وَ لَمْ یَدَعْهَا مُتَعَمِّداً فَتَحَ اللَّهُ لَهُ ثَمَانِیَهَ أَبْوَابٍ مِنَ الْجَنَّهِ یَدْخُلُ مِنْ أَیِّهَا شَاءَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to us by Sa’d b. Abdillah from Ahmad b. Musa from al-Hussain b. Sa’eed from Ibn Abi ’Umayr from Muawiyah b. Wahab from ’Amru b. Yazid from Salim al-Makki from Abi Ja’far (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “If he appears on the Day of Judgment without leaving these actions purposely after every prayer Allah will open all eight doors of Paradise for him and he will be free to enter Paradise from whichever door he wishes.”

records.chapter: Reward of controlling oneself at the time of fear and anger
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. It was narrated to me by Ahmad b. Muhammad from Sa’d b. Abdillah from Muhammad b. Isa from al-Hassan b. Ali b. Fadhal from Ghalib b. Uthman from Shu’aib from a man from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: “One who controls himself at the time of fear and anger, Allah will prohibit fire for his body.”
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ سَعْدِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عِیسَی عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ عَلِیِّ بْنِ فَضَّالٍ عَنْ غَالِبِ بْنِ عُثْمَانَ عَنْ شُعَیْبٍ عَنْ رَجُلٍ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: مَنْ مَلَکَ نَفْسَهُ إِذَا رَغِبَ وَ إِذَا رَهِبَ وَ إِذَا اشْتَهَی وَ إِذَا غَضِبَ حَرَّمَ اللَّهُ جَسَدَهُ عَلَی النَّارِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. It was narrated to me by Ahmad b. Muhammad from Sa’d b. Abdillah from Muhammad b. Isa from al-Hassan b. Ali b. Fadhal from Ghalib b. Uthman from Shu’aib from a man from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “One who controls himself at the time of fear and anger, Allah will prohibit fire for his body.”

records.chapter: Reward of helping in Amr bil Maroof and Nahi Anil Munkar
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1.  Imam Muhammad Baqir (a.s.) says, “Amr bin Maroof and Nahi Anil Munkar are the creatures of Allah, the Mighty and Sublime. One who helps them has honored Allah. One who insults  them, Allah,  the  Mighty  and Sublime  will  disgrace him.”
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ مُوسَی بْنِ الْمُتَوَکِّلِ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ یَحْیَی الْعَطَّارُ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَحْمَدَ عَنْ یَعْقُوبَ بْنِ یَزِیدَ یَرْفَعُهُ قَالَ قَالَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ ع الْآمِرُ بِالْمَعْرُوفِ وَ النَّاهِی عَنِ الْمُنْکَرِ خَلْقَانِ مِنْ خَلْقِ اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ فَمَنْ نَصَرَهُمَا أَعَزَّهُ اللَّهُ وَ مَنْ خَذَلَهُمَا خَذَلَهُ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ.

records.chapter: Reward of supplicating in congregation
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. Ali Majluwayh who said it was narrated to me by my uncle Muhammad b. Abi al-Qasim from Ahmad b. Abi Abdillah al-Barqi from Muhammad b. Ali from Yunis b. Ya’qub from Abd al-A’la from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: “If four people gather and supplicate together their supplication is accepted before they disperse.”
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَلِیٍّ مَاجِیلَوَیْهِ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی عَمِّی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ أَبِی الْقَاسِمِ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ الْبَرْقِیِّ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَلِیٍّ عَنْ یُونُسَ بْنِ یَعْقُوبَ عَنْ عَبْدِ الْأَعْلَی عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: مَا اجْتَمَعَ أَرْبَعَهٌ قَطُّ عَلَی أَمْرٍ وَاحِدٍ یدعوا [فَدَعَوْا] إِلَّا تَفَرَّقُوا عَنْ إِجَابَهٍ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. Ali Majluwayh who said it was narrated to me by my uncle Muhammad b. Abi al-Qasim from Ahmad b. Abi Abdillah al-Barqi from Muhammad b. Ali from Yunis b. Ya’qub from Abd al-A’la from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “If four people gather and supplicate together their supplication is accepted before they disperse.”

records.chapter: Reward of supplicating secretly
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. al-Hassan who said it was narrated to me by Muhammad b. al-Hassan al-Saffar from Ahmad b. Muhammad from Abi Humam Ismail b. Humam from Abi al-Hassan al-Ridha (a.s.) who said: “A supplication done secretly is better than seventy supplications done openly.”
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ الصَّفَّارُ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ أَبِی هَمَّامٍ إِسْمَاعِیلَ بْنِ هَمَّامٍ عَنْ أَبِی الْحَسَنِ الرِّضَا ع قَالَ: دَعْوَهُ الْعَبْدِ سِرّاً دَعْوَهً وَاحِدَهً تَعْدِلُ سَبْعِینَ دَعْوَهً عَلَانِیَهً.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. al-Hassan who said it was narrated to me by Muhammad b. al-Hassan al-Saffar from Ahmad b. Muhammad from Abi Humam Ismail b. Humam from Abi al-Hassan al-Ridha (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “A supplication done secretly is better than seventy supplications done openly.”

records.chapter: Reward of supplication before dawn
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. Musa b. al-Mutawakil who said it was narrated to me by Ali b. al-Hussain al-Sa’dabadi from Ahmad b. Abi Abdillah al-Barqi who said it was narrated to me by Abu Abdillah al-Jamurani from al-Hassan b. Ali b. Hamzah al-Bata’ini from Mandal b. Ali from Abi al-Sabah al-Kanani from Abi Ja’far (a.s.) who said: “Allah loves all the supplications of a believer. I advise you to supplicate between dawn and sunrise because the doors of heaven are opened, breeze of mercy blows, sustenance is distributed and greatest wishes are fulfilled during that time.”
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ مُوسَی بْنِ الْمُتَوَکِّلِ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی عَلِیُّ بْنُ الْحُسَیْنِ السَّعْدَآبَادِیُّ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ الْبَرْقِیِّ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ الْجَامُورَانِیُّ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ عَلِیِّ بْنِ حَمْزَهَ الْبَطَائِنِیِّ عَنْ مَنْدَلِ بْنِ عَلِیٍّ عَنْ أَبِی الصَّبَّاحِ الْکِنَانِیِّ عَنْ أَبِی جَعْفَرٍ ع قَالَ: إِنَّ اللَّهَ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ یُحِبُّ مِنْ عِبَادِهِ الْمُؤْمِنِینَ کُلَّ دَعَّاءٍ فَعَلَیْکُمْ بِالدُّعَاءِ فِی السَّحَرِ إِلَی طُلُوعِ الشَّمْسِ فَإِنَّهَا سَاعَهٌ تُفْتَحُ فِیهَا أَبْوَابُ السَّمَاءِ وَ تَهُبُّ الرِّیَاحُ وَ تُقَسَّمُ فِیهَا الْأَرْزَاقُ وَ تُقْضَی فِیهَا الْحَوَائِجُ الْعِظَامُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. Musa b. al-Mutawakil who said it was narrated to me by Ali b. al-Hussain al-Sa’dabadi from Ahmad b. Abi Abdillah al-Barqi who said it was narrated to me by Abu Abdillah al-Jamurani from al-Hassan b. Ali b. Hamzah al-Bata’ini from Mandal b. Ali from Abi al-Sabah al-Kanani from Abi Ja’far (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “Allah loves all the supplications of a believer. I advise you to supplicate between dawn and sunrise because the doors of heaven are opened, breeze of mercy blows, sustenance is distributed and greatest wishes are fulfilled during that time.”

records.chapter: Reward of supplicating for Believing men and women and Muslim men and women
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1.  Imam Musa Kadhim (a.s.) said, “One who supplicates for believers,  the  Almighty  Allah  appoints  an  angel  for  each believer to supplicate for that person.”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا سَعْدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ یَعْقُوبَ بْنِ یَزِیدَ عَنْ صَفْوَانَ بْنِ یَحْیَی عَنْ أَبِی الْحَسَنِ ع أَنَّهُ کَانَ یَقُولُ مَنْ دَعَا لِإِخْوَانِهِ مِنَ الْمُؤْمِنِینَ وَ الْمُؤْمِنَاتِ وَ الْمُسْلِمِینَ وَ الْمُسْلِمَاتِ وَکَّلَ اللَّهُ بِهِ عَنْ کُلِّ مُؤْمِنٍ مَلَکاً یَدْعُو لَهُ.

records.chapter: Reward of supplicating for Believing men and women and Muslim men and women
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 2. And from the previous chain from Abi al-Hassan al-Ridha (a.s.) who said: “No believer supplicates for a living or dead believing men and women and Muslim men and women but that the Almighty Allah writes for him the reward of the good deeds of all believers from Adam (a.s.) till Qiyamat.”
records.arabicText: وَ بِهَذَا الْإِسْنَادِ عَنْ أَبِی الْحَسَنِ الرِّضَا ع قَالَ: مَا مِنْ مُؤْمِنٍ یَدْعُو لِلْمُؤْمِنِینَ وَ الْمُؤْمِنَاتِ وَ الْمُسْلِمِینَ وَ الْمُسْلِمَاتِ الْأَحْیَاءِ مِنْهُمْ وَ الْأَمْوَاتِ إِلَّا کَتَبَ اللَّهُ لَهُ بِکُلِّ مُؤْمِنٍ وَ مُؤْمِنَهٍ حَسَنَهً مُنْذُ بَعَثَ اللَّهُ آدَمَ إِلَی أَنْ تَقُومَ السَّاعَهُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 2. And from the previous chain from Abi al-Hassan al-Ridha (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “No believer supplicates for a living or dead believing men and women and Muslim men and women but that the Almighty Allah writes for him the reward of the good deeds of all believers from Adam (a.s.) till Qiyamat.”

records.chapter: Reward of supplicating for Believing men and women and Muslim men and women
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 3. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. al-Hassan who said it was narrated to me by Muhammad b. al-Hassan al-Saffar from Ahmad b. Abi Abdillah who said it was narrated to me by Abi from Ali b. al-Nu’man from Fadhl b. Yusif from Abdillah b. Sinan from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: “One who recites twenty five times everyday: Allaahummagh fir lil mu-mineena wal mu- minaati wal muslimeena wal muslimaat the Almighty Allah will write for him the reward of all the believers who passed away till those are yet to be born and wipe his sins out and increase his rank.”
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ الصَّفَّارُ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی أَبِی عَنْ عَلِیِّ بْنِ النُّعْمَانِ عَنْ فَضْلِ بْنِ یُوسُفَ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ سِنَانٍ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: مَنْ قَالَ کُلَّ یَوْمٍ خَمْسَاً وَ عِشْرِینَ مَرَّهً اللَّهُمَّ اغْفِرْ لِلْمُؤْمِنِینَ وَ الْمُؤْمِنَاتِ وَ الْمُسْلِمِینَ وَ الْمُسْلِمَاتِ کَتَبَ اللَّهُ لَهُ بِعَدَدِ کُلِّ مُؤْمِنٍ مَضَی وَ کُلِّ مُؤْمِنٍ بَقِیَ إِلَی یَوْمِ الْقِیَامَهِ حَسَنَهً وَ مَحَا عَنْهُ سَیِّئَهً وَ رَفَعَ لَهُ دَرَجَهً.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 3. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. al-Hassan who said it was narrated to me by Muhammad b. al-Hassan al-Saffar from Ahmad b. Abi Abdillah who said it was narrated to me by Abi from Ali b. al-Nu’man from Fadhl b. Yusif from Abdillah b. Sinan from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “One who recites twenty five times everyday: Allaahummagh fir lil mu-mineena wal mu- minaati wal muslimeena wal muslimaat the Almighty Allah will write for him the reward of all the believers who passed away till those are yet to be born and wipe his sins out and increase his rank.”

records.chapter: Reward of supplicating for Believing men and women and Muslim men and women
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 4.  Imam Ja’far Sadiq (a.s.) narrates from his predecessors that the  Holy  Prophet  (s.a.w.s.)  said,  “No  one  supplicates  for believing men or women except that the Almighty Allah will accept the same supplication for him corresponding to the number of believers who passed away until those who will be born till the Day of Judgment. If an order is given to take that person to Paradise those believers will call out, ‘O Lord! He is the same person who supplicated for us. We stand to save him’. The Almighty Allah will accept their intercession and save him from the fire (of Hell).”
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَلِیٍّ مَاجِیلَوَیْهِ عَنْ عَمِّهِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِی الْقَاسِمِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَلِیٍّ الْکُوفِیِّ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ الْحَسَنِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ حَمَّادٍ الحارفی [الْحَارِثِیِ] عَنْ أَبِیهِ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ص مَا مِنْ عَبْدٍ دَعَا لِلْمُؤْمِنِینَ وَ الْمُؤْمِنَاتِ إِلَّا رَدَّ اللَّهُ عَلَیْهِ مِثْلَ الَّذِی دَعَا لَهُمْ مِنْ کُلِّ مُؤْمِنٍ وَ مُؤْمِنَهٍ مَضَی مِنْ أَوَّلِ الدَّهْرِ وَ هُوَ آتٍ إِلَی یَوْمِ الْقِیَامَهِ وَ إِنَّ الْعَبْدَ لَیُؤْمَرُ بِهِ إِلَی النَّارِ یُسْحَبُ فَیَقُولُ الْمُؤْمِنُونَ وَ الْمُؤْمِنَاتُ یَا رَبَّنَا هَذَا الَّذِی کَانَ یَدْعُو لَنَا فَشَفِّعْنَا فِیهِ فَیُشَفِّعُهُمُ اللَّهُ فِیهِ فَیَنْجُو مِنَ النَّارِ.

records.chapter: Reward of supplicating for Believing men and women and Muslim men and women
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 5.  Imam  Ja’far  Sadiq  (a.s.)  narrates  that  the  Holy  Prophet (s.a.w.s.) said, “When one of you supplicates it should be for everyone in general, because it a factor for acceptance of supplications.”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی عَلِیُّ بْنُ إِبْرَاهِیمَ عَنْ أَبِیهِ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ مَیْمُونٍ الْقَدَّاحِ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ص إِذَا دَعَا أَحَدُکُمْ فَلْیَعُمَّ فَإِنَّهُ أَوْجَبُ لِلدُّعَاءِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 5.  Imam  Ja’far  Sadiq  (a.s.)  narrates  that  the  Holy  Prophet (s.a.w.s.) said,
records.thaqalaynMatn: “When one of you supplicates it should be for everyone in general, because it a factor for acceptance of supplications.”

records.chapter: Reward of reciting Laa h’aula wa laa Quwwata illa billaah
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1.  It is narrated that Imam Ja’far Sadiq (a.s.) said, “Allah, the Mighty and Sublime will remove seventy types of calamities from  the  one  who says  ‘Laa  h’aula  wa  laa  quwwata  illaa billaahil a’liyyil a’z’eem’ the least of which is death due to suffocation.”
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ الْحَسَنِ الصَّفَّارِ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ الْحَکَمِ عَنِ الْحُسَیْنِ بْنِ سَیْفِ بْنِ عَمِیرَهَ عَنْ هِشَامِ بْنِ سَالِمٍ قَالَ سَمِعْتُ أَبَا الْحَسَنِ الرِّضَا ع کَانَ یَقُولُ مَنْ قَالَ لَا حَوْلَ وَ لَا قُوَّهَ إِلَّا بِاللَّهِ الْعَلِیِّ الْعَظِیمِ دَفَعَ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ بِهَا عَنْهُ سَبْعِینَ نَوْعاً مِنَ الْبَلَاءِ أَیْسَرُهَا الْخَنْقُ.

records.chapter: Reward of reciting Laa h’aula wa laa Quwwata illa billaah daily
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Sa’d b. Abdillah from Ibrahim b. Hashim from ’Amru b. Uthman from Muhammad b. Adhafir from ’Amru b. Yazid from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: “Allah will remove seventy types of calamities from the one who says a hundred times everyday: ‘Laa h’aula wa laa quwwata illaa billaah’ the least of which is sorrow.”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی سَعْدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ إِبْرَاهِیمَ بْنِ هَاشِمٍ عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ عُثْمَانَ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عُذَافِرٍ عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ یَزِیدَ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: مَنْ قَالَ فِی کُلِّ یَوْمٍ مِائَهَ مَرَّهٍ لَا حَوْلَ وَ لَا قُوَّهَ إِلَّا بِاللَّهِ دَفَعَ اللَّهُ بِهَا عَنْهُ سَبْعِینَ نَوْعاً مِنَ الْبَلَاءِ أَیْسَرُهَا الْهَمُّ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Sa’d b. Abdillah from Ibrahim b. Hashim from ’Amru b. Uthman from Muhammad b. Adhafir from ’Amru b. Yazid from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “Allah will remove seventy types of calamities from the one who says a hundred times everyday: ‘Laa h’aula wa laa quwwata illaa billaah’ the least of which is sorrow.”

records.chapter: Reward of saying Bismillaahi wa laa h’aula wa laa Quwwata illa billaah coming out of house
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. al-Hassan who said it was narrated to me by Muhammad b. al-Hassan al-Saffar from Muawiyah b. Hakeem from Ibn Abi ’Umayr from Aban b. Uthman from Muhammad b. Sa’eed from ’Atiyyah al-’Ufa from Abi Sa’eed al-Khudri from The Prophet (s.a.w.s.) who said: ‘How can I misguide a person who is guided, protected and needless?’”
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ الصَّفَّارُ عَنْ مُعَاوِیَهَ بْنِ حُکَیْمٍ عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِی عُمَیْرٍ عَنْ أَبَانِ بْنِ عُثْمَانَ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ سَعِیدٍ عَنْ عَطِیَّهَ الْعَوْفِیِّ عَنْ أَبِی سَعِیدٍ الْخُدْرِیِّ عَنِ النَّبِیِّ ص قَالَ: مَنْ قَالَ إِذَا خَرَجَ مِنْ بَیْتِهِ بِاسْمِ اللَّهِ قَالَ الْمَلَکَانِ هُدِیتَ فَإِنْ قَالَ لَا حَوْلَ وَ لَا قُوَّهَ إِلَّا بِاللَّهِ قَالا وُقِیتَ فَإِنْ قَالَ تَوَکَّلْتُ عَلَی اللَّهِ قَالا کُفِیتَ فَیَقُولُ الشَّیْطَانُ کَیْفَ لِی بِعَبْدٍ هُدِیَ وَ وُقِیَ وَ کُفِیَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. al-Hassan who said it was narrated to me by Muhammad b. al-Hassan al-Saffar from Muawiyah b. Hakeem from Ibn Abi ’Umayr from Aban b. Uthman from Muhammad b. Sa’eed from ’Atiyyah al-’Ufa from Abi Sa’eed al-Khudri from The Prophet (s.a.w.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: ‘How can I misguide a person who is guided, protected and needless?’”

records.chapter: Reward of reciting Takbir a hundred times at night
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1.  Imam Zainul Abideen (a.s.) said, “One who recites Takbir a  hundred  times  in  the  evening  is  like  one  who  has emancipated a hundred slaves.”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ یَحْیَی بْنِ أَحْمَدَ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ الْحُسَیْنِ اللُّؤْلُؤِیِّ عَنْ عَلِیِّ بْنِ النُّعْمَانِ عَنْ یَحْیَی بْنِ زَکَرِیَّا عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ رِبَاطٍ عَنْ أَبِی حَمْزَهَ الثُّمَالِیِّ قَالَ سَمِعْتُ عَلِیَّ بْنَ الْحُسَیْنِ ع یَقُولُ مَنْ کَبَّرَ اللَّهَ عِنْدَ الْمَسَاءِ مِائَهَ تَکْبِیرَهٍ کَانَ کَمَنْ عتق [أَعْتَقَ] مِائَهَ نَسَمَهٍ.

records.chapter: Reward of reciting Tasbih of Fatima Zahra (s.a.)
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1.  Imam Ja’far Sadiq (a.s.) told Abu Haroon Makfuf, “O Abu Haroon! We advise our children to recite Tasbih of Zahra (s.a.) much in the same way as we do to offer Salaat. You too  recite  this  Tasbih  for  one  who  does  not  recite  it,  is unfortunate.”
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ الْحَسَنِ الصَّفَّارِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ الْحُسَیْنِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ إِسْمَاعِیلَ عَنْ صَالِحِ بْنِ عُقْبَهَ عَنْ أَبِی هَارُونَ الْمَکْفُوفِ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ لِأَبِی هَارُونَ الْمَکْفُوفِ یَا أَبَا هَارُونَ إِنَّا نَأْمُرُ صِبْیَانَنَا بِتَسْبِیحِ الزَّهْرَاءِ ع کَمَا نَأْمُرُهُمْ بِالصَّلَاهِ فَالْزَمْهُ فَإِنَّهُ لَمْ یَلْزَمْهُ عَبْدٌ فَیَشْقَی.

records.chapter: Reward of reciting Tasbih of Fatima Zahra (s.a.)
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 2.  Imam  Muhammad  Baqir  (a.s.)  said,  “One  who  seeks forgiveness  after  reciting  Tasbih  of  Zahra  (s.a.)  will  be forgiven. Although it is recited a hundred times, it is equal to one  thousand  Tasbih  in  Mizan.  It  keeps  Satan  away  and pleases the Merciful.”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ یَحْیَی عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَحْمَدَ عَنْ أَبِی جَعْفَرِ بْنِ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ سَعِیدٍ الْبَجَلِیِّ ابْنِ أَخِی صَفْوَانَ بْنِ یَحْیَی عَنْ عَلِیِّ بْنِ أَسْبَاطٍ عَنْ سَیْفِ بْنِ عَمِیرَهَ عَنْ أَبِی الصَّبَّاحِ بْنِ نُعَیْمٍ الْعَائِذِیِّ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ قَالَ قَالَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ ع مَنْ سَبَّحَ تَسْبِیحَ الزَّهْرَاءِ ع ثُمَّ اسْتَغْفَرَ غُفِرَ لَهُ وَ هِیَ مِائَهٌ بِاللِّسَانِ وَ أَلْفٌ فِی الْمِیزَانِ وَ تَطْرُدُ الشَّیْطَانَ وَ تُرْضِی الرَّحْمَنَ.

records.chapter: Reward of reciting Tasbih of Fatima Zahra (s.a.)
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 3.  It  is  narrated  that  Imam  Ja’far  Sadiq  (a.s.)  said,  “I  like reciting this Tasbih every day after Salaat more than offering a thousand rakats of prayer daily.”
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ الْحَسَنِ الصَّفَّارِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ الْحَسَنِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ إِسْمَاعِیلَ عَنْ أَبِی خَلَفٍ الْقَمَّاطِ قَالَ سَمِعْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع یَقُولُ تَسْبِیحُ فَاطِمَهَ الزَّهْرَاءِ ع فِی کُلِّ یَوْمٍ فِی دُبُرِ کُلِّ صَلَاهٍ أَحَبُّ إِلَیَّ مِنْ صَلَاهِ أَلْفِ رَکْعَهٍ فِی کُلِّ یَوْمٍ.

records.chapter: Reward of reciting Tasbih of Fatima Zahra (s.a.)
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 4.  Imam  Ja’far  Sadiq  (a.s.)  said,  “Allah  forgives  one  who recites Tasbih of Zahra (s.a.) before getting up after Salaat. One should begin it by reciting Takbeer (Allaahu Akbar).”
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی الْحُسَیْنُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ أَبَانٍ عَنِ الْحُسَیْنِ بْنِ سَعِیدٍ عَنْ فَضَالَهَ بْنِ أَبِی نَجْرَانَ عَنْ سِنَانٍ قَالَ قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع مَنْ سَبَّحَ تَسْبِیحَ فَاطِمَهَ ع قَبْلَ أَنْ یَثْنِیَ رِجْلَیْهِ مِنْ صَلَاتِهِ الْفَرِیضَهِ غَفَرَ اللَّهُ لَهُ وَ یَبْدَأُ بِالتَّکْبِیرِ.

records.chapter: Reward of silence
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Muhammad b. Idris from Muhammad b. Ahmad from Musa b. ’Imran from Ali b. al-Hussain b. Ribat from some of his men from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: “A believer is counted among virtuous till he is silent. As soon as he speaks, he is included either among good people or bad ones.”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ إِدْرِیسَ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَحْمَدَ عَنْ مُوسَی بْنِ عِمْرَانَ عَنْ عَلِیِّ بْنِ الْحُسَیْنِ بْنِ رِبَاطٍ عَنْ بَعْضِ رِجَالِهِ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: لَا یَزَالُ الْعَبْدُ الْمُؤْمِنُ یُکْتَبُ مُحْسِناً مَا دَامَ سَاکِتاً فَإِذَا تَکَلَّمَ کُتِبَ مُحْسِناً أَوْ مُسِیئاً.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Muhammad b. Idris from Muhammad b. Ahmad from Musa b. ’Imran from Ali b. al-Hussain b. Ribat from some of his men from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “A believer is counted among virtuous till he is silent. As soon as he speaks, he is included either among good people or bad ones.”

records.chapter: Reward of repenting
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1.  The  Holy  Prophet  (s.a.w.s.)  said,  “There  is  a  cure  for every malady and the cure of sins is repentance.”
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ الصَّفَّارُ عَنِ الْعَبَّاسِ بْنِ مَعْرُوفٍ عَنِ النَّوْفَلِیِّ عَنِ السَّکُونِیِّ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ أَبِیهِ عَنْ آبَائِهِ ع قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ص لِکُلِّ دَاءٍ دَوَاءٌ وَ دَوَاءُ الذُّنُوبِ الِاسْتِغْفَارُ.

records.chapter: Reward of repenting
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 2. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Sa’d b. Abdillah from al-Hussain b. Ali from Isa b. Hisham from Salam al-Hannat from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: “One who seeks the forgiveness of Allah a hundred times at bedtime all his sins are destroyed like dry leaves of a tree fall off and none of his sins remains in the morning.”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی سَعْدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنِ الْحُسَیْنِ بْنِ عَلِیٍّ عَنْ عِیسَی بْنِ هِشَامٍ عَنْ سَلَّامٍ الْحَنَّاطِ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: مَنِ اسْتَغْفَرَ اللَّهَ مِائَهَ مَرَّهٍ حِینَ یَنَامُ بَاتَ وَ قَدْ تَحَاتَّتِ الذُّنُوبُ کُلُّهَا عَنْهُ کَمَا تَتَحَاتُّ الْوَرَقُ مِنَ الشَّجَرِ وَ یُصْبِحُ وَ لَیْسَ عَلَیْهِ ذَنْبٌ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 2. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Sa’d b. Abdillah from al-Hussain b. Ali from Isa b. Hisham from Salam al-Hannat from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “One who seeks the forgiveness of Allah a hundred times at bedtime all his sins are destroyed like dry leaves of a tree fall off and none of his sins remains in the morning.”

records.chapter: Reward of repenting
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 3.  It is narrated from Imam Muhammad Baqir (a.s.) that the Holy Prophet (s.a.w.s.) said, “My presence among you and your  repentance  is  a  strong  fortress  to  remain  safe  from chastisement.” Imam said, “Now our great protection is no longer  with  the  world  and  the  only  thing  remaining  is repentance.  So  repent  a  lot  for  it  wipes  sins  as  Almighty Allah says: But Allah was not going to chastise them while you were among them, nor is Allah going to chastise them while yet they ask for forgiveness.”15
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَلِیٍّ مَاجِیلَوَیْهِ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ یَحْیَی عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَحْمَدَ عَنْ مُوسَی بْنِ جَعْفَرٍ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ عَلِیِّ بْنِ نُوحٍ عَنْ صَالِحِ بْنِ عُقْبَهَ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ الْجُعْفِیِّ عَنْ أَبِی جَعْفَرٍ ع قَالَ سَمِعْتُهُ یَقُولُ کَانَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ص یَقُولُ مُقَامِی فِیکُمْ وَ الِاسْتِغْفَارُ لَکُمْ حِصْنٌ حَصِینٌ مِنَ الْعَذَابِ فَمَضَی أَکْثَرُ الْحِصْنَیْنِ وَ بَقِیَ الِاسْتِغْفَارُ فَأَکْثِرُوا مِنْهُ فَإِنَّهُ مَمْحَاهٌ لِلذُّنُوبِ قَالَ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ- وَ ما کانَ اللَّهُ لِیُعَذِّبَهُمْ وَ أَنْتَ فِیهِمْ وَ ما کانَ اللَّهُ مُعَذِّبَهُمْ وَ هُمْ یَسْتَغْفِرُونَ
records.thaqalaynSanad: 3.  It is narrated from Imam Muhammad Baqir (a.s.) that the Holy Prophet (s.a.w.s.) said,
records.thaqalaynMatn: “My presence among you and your  repentance  is  a  strong  fortress  to  remain  safe  from chastisement.” Imam said, “Now our great protection is no longer  with  the  world  and  the  only  thing  remaining  is repentance.  So  repent  a  lot  for  it  wipes  sins  as  Almighty Allah says: But Allah was not going to chastise them while you were among them, nor is Allah going to chastise them while yet they ask for forgiveness.”15

records.chapter: Reward of repenting
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 4. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Sa’d b. Abdillah from al-Haytham b. Abi Masruq al-Hindi from Ismail b. Sahl who said: The narrator wrote to Imam Muhammad Baqir (a.s.) to let him know those deeds, which will keep him along with Imam (a.s.) in this world and the hereafter. Imam (a.s.) replied, “Recite Innaa anzalnaa (Surah Qadr) extensively and your tongue should not stop from repenting.”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی سَعْدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنِ الْهَیْثَمِ بْنِ أَبِی مَسْرُوقٍ النَّهْدِیِّ عَنْ إِسْمَاعِیلَ بْنِ سَهْلٍ قَالَ: کَتَبْتُ إِلَی أَبِی جَعْفَرٍ ع عَلِّمْنِی شَیْئاً إِذَا أَنَا قُلْتُهُ کُنْتُ مَعَکُمْ فِی الدُّنْیَا وَ الْآخِرَهِ قَالَ فَکَتَبَ بِخَطٍّ أَعْرِفُهُ أَکْثِرْ مِنْ تِلَاوَهِ إِنَّا أَنْزَلْنَاهُ وَ رَطِّبْ شَفَتَیْکَ بِالاسْتِغْفَارِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 4. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Sa’d b. Abdillah from al-Haytham b. Abi Masruq al-Hindi from Ismail b. Sahl who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: The narrator wrote to Imam Muhammad Baqir (a.s.) to let him know those deeds, which will keep him along with Imam (a.s.) in this world and the hereafter. Imam (a.s.) replied, “Recite Innaa anzalnaa (Surah Qadr) extensively and your tongue should not stop from repenting.”
